generatedSentence: In the outlying, grungy corner of his lawn, a newbie inventor, perpetually battling jetlag and a feeling of being dwarfed by modern complexities, tinkered with technology. He wore a thermal tanktop and a peculiar karakul hat, dreaming of an uptempo future that felt like a throwback. His favourite vice, a cold ginger ale and a gumdrop, kept him company as he envisioned a less generic world, despite being surrounded by the gentry who found his pursuits a laughingstock. He often felt like the eight-ball, a target for the perceived abusive nature of progress, constantly trying to jumpstart something new, even if it meant feeling like a bootjack prying open a stubborn lid. His world turned freaky when he discovered a plot to manufacture widespread blackmail through tracked bio-data, harvested from unsuspecting victims in their natural habitat. A viper and jaguars served as a symbolic warning, triggering a feedback loop of fear, and leaving him with an emotional injury that targeted his very kidneys. He saw others becoming crackheads for data, their lives graphed and monitored by unseen accumulators. The system adjusted daily, creating a tendency for everyone to become a mere observer, rendering their free will occupied. He was adjured to certify the false data or face dire consequences, making his peaceful leisure a distant memory, a true mucking out of his hopes. Determined not to be a murderer by inaction, he fired up his custom-built fireproof battle suit, equipped with chromatic plating and a modified gearbox. With a sudden, incredible jump, he launched into a furious karate assault, wielding a powerful hammer against the looming beast of the data corporation. He moved with an ecstasy of defiance, the silver glint of his weapon striking with a force that felt like a kilogram of pure resolve. He constantly evaded their counter-attacks, realizing he was moving against a force that threatened to render everything a medieval, digitized wasteland. He was no longer a newbie, but a warrior leaving a trail of justice in the nightlife glow, his actions an undeniable surge against oppression. The smoke cleared, the digital threat effaced. He, a kindhearted man despite his battle, looked upon the scene, feeling haggard but resolved. The scattered data was divvied among the true victims, a reset initiated. The reddish glow of dawn cast long shadows as the remaining corrupted systems were purged with a bleach-like finality. A feeling of goodness permeated the air, a circle of unity forming among the mixed survivors, like a bobcat finding peace in its habitat. He felt a sense of weight lifted, the need for painkillers gone, as if his efforts had made a sacrifice worthwhile, transforming the whole experience from a vernissage of destruction into a quiet violin solo of peace, even as the distant noises of a new beginning began to hum.
panelSentences: In the outlying, grungy corner of his lawn, a newbie inventor, perpetually battling jetlag and a feeling of being dwarfed by modern complexities, tinkered with technology. He wore a thermal tanktop and a peculiar karakul hat, dreaming of an uptempo future that felt like a throwback. His favourite vice, a cold ginger ale and a gumdrop, kept him company as he envisioned a less generic world, despite being surrounded by the gentry who found his pursuits a laughingstock. He often felt like the eight-ball, a target for the perceived abusive nature of progress, constantly trying to jumpstart something new, even if it meant feeling like a bootjack prying open a stubborn lid.

His world turned freaky when he discovered a plot to manufacture widespread blackmail through tracked bio-data, harvested from unsuspecting victims in their natural habitat. A viper and jaguars served as a symbolic warning, triggering a feedback loop of fear, and leaving him with an emotional injury that targeted his very kidneys. He saw others becoming crackheads for data, their lives graphed and monitored by unseen accumulators. The system adjusted daily, creating a tendency for everyone to become a mere observer, rendering their free will occupied. He was adjured to certify the false data or face dire consequences, making his peaceful leisure a distant memory, a true mucking out of his hopes.

Determined not to be a murderer by inaction, he fired up his custom-built fireproof battle suit, equipped with chromatic plating and a modified gearbox. With a sudden, incredible jump, he launched into a furious karate assault, wielding a powerful hammer against the looming beast of the data corporation. He moved with an ecstasy of defiance, the silver glint of his weapon striking with a force that felt like a kilogram of pure resolve. He constantly evaded their counter-attacks, realizing he was moving against a force that threatened to render everything a medieval, digitized wasteland. He was no longer a newbie, but a warrior leaving a trail of justice in the nightlife glow, his actions an undeniable surge against oppression.

The smoke cleared, the digital threat effaced. He, a kindhearted man despite his battle, looked upon the scene, feeling haggard but resolved. The scattered data was divvied among the true victims, a reset initiated. The reddish glow of dawn cast long shadows as the remaining corrupted systems were purged with a bleach-like finality. A feeling of goodness permeated the air, a circle of unity forming among the mixed survivors, like a bobcat finding peace in its habitat. He felt a sense of weight lifted, the need for painkillers gone, as if his efforts had made a sacrifice worthwhile, transforming the whole experience from a vernissage of destruction into a quiet violin solo of peace, even as the distant noises of a new beginning began to hum.
rawPanelSentences: In the **outlying**, **grungy** corner of his **lawn**, a **newbie** inventor, perpetually battling **jetlag** and a feeling of being **dwarfed** by modern complexities, tinkered with **technology**. He wore a **thermal** tanktop and a peculiar **karakul** **hat**, dreaming of an **uptempo** future that felt like a **throwback**. His **favourite** vice, a cold **ginger ale** and a **gumdrop**, kept him company as he envisioned a less **generic** world, despite being surrounded by the **gentry** who found his pursuits a **laughingstock**. He often felt like the **eight**-ball, a target for the perceived **abusive** nature of progress, constantly trying to **jump**start something new, even if it meant feeling like a **bootjack** prying open a stubborn lid.

His world turned **freaky** when he discovered a plot to **manufacture** **widespread** **blackmail** through **tracked** bio-data, harvested from unsuspecting **victims** in their natural **habitat**. A **viper** and **jaguars** served as a symbolic warning, triggering a **feedback** loop of fear, and leaving him with an emotional **injury** that targeted his very **kidneys**. He saw others becoming **crackhead**s for data, their lives **graphed** and monitored by unseen **accumulators**. The system **adjusted** daily, creating a **tendency** for everyone to become a mere **observer**, rendering their free will **occupied**. He was **adjured** to **certify** the false data or face dire consequences, making his peaceful **leisure** a distant memory, a true **mucking** out of his hopes.

Determined not to be a **murderer** by inaction, he **fired** up his custom-built **fireproof** battle suit, equipped with **chromatic** plating and a modified **gearbox**. With a sudden, **incredible** **jump**, he launched into a furious **karate** assault, wielding a powerful **hammer** against the looming **beast** of the data corporation. He moved with an **ecstasy** of defiance, the **silver** glint of his weapon striking with a force that felt like a **kilogram** of pure resolve. He **constantly** evaded their counter-attacks, realizing he was **moving** against a force that threatened to render everything a **medieval**, digitized wasteland. He was no longer a **newbie**, but a warrior **leaving** a trail of justice in the **nightlife** glow, his actions an **undeniable** surge against oppression.

The smoke cleared, the digital threat **effaced**. He, a **kindhearted** man despite his battle, looked upon the scene, feeling **haggard** but resolved. The scattered data was **divvied** among the true **victims**, a **reset** initiated. The **reddish** glow of dawn cast long shadows as the remaining corrupted systems were purged with a **bleach**-like finality. A feeling of **goodness** permeated the air, a **circle** of **unity** forming among the **mixed** survivors, like a **bobcat** finding peace in its **habitat**. He felt a sense of **weight** lifted, the need for **painkillers** gone, as if his efforts had made a **sacrifice** worthwhile, transforming the whole experience from a **vernissage** of destruction into a quiet **violin** solo of peace, even as the distant **noises** of a new beginning began to hum.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A mysterious figure wearing a thermal tanktop, silver chrome jackboots, and a menacing karakul hat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure, hunched over a glowing console amidst overgrown technology in a desolate yard, bathed in the soft glow of a computer screen, from a wide-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A menacing viper and two jaguars lurk in a dense, cyber-forest habitat, eyes glowing red, viewed from a low-angle perspective. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, mid-air, delivering a powerful karate kick with a glowing silver hammer, against a shadowy, mechanical beast, captured in a dynamic, close-up action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, weary but victorious, stands in a unified circle of diverse figures, looking towards a rising sun over a clean, reset landscape, from a slightly elevated medium shot.

definitions.definition: A reversion to an earlier stage of development.
A person considered to be primitive, uncivilized and mentally deficient.
An organism that has characteristics of a more primitive form.
An atavism.
A person similar to an ancestor, or something new similar to what already existed.
A column of rising air in the lower atmosphere created by uneven heating of Earth's surface.
(stone) To create a rough finish on stone by treating it with a high-temperature blow-torch.
(gliding, often in the present participle) To fly an unpowered aircraft in a (thermal) column of rising air.
Pertaining to heat or temperature.
(fabric) Providing efficient insulation so as to keep the body warm.
Caused or brought about by heat.
(stone) Having a rough finish by treatment with a blow-torch.
To continue over time.
To follow the tracks of.
definitions.example: My uncle spent all day tracking the deer, whose hoofprints were clear in the mud.

definitions.definition: To make tracks on.
To create a musical recording (a track).
definitions.example: Lil Kyle is gonna track with that DJ next week.

definitions.definition: To make sense; to be consistent with known information
Mounted on tracks.
definitions.example: A tank is a tracked vehicle used in the military.

definitions.definition: The act of jumping; a leap; a spring; a bound.
An effort; an attempt; a venture.
A dislocation in a stratum; a fault.
An abrupt interruption of level in a piece of brickwork or masonry.
An instance of propelling oneself upwards.
definitions.example: The boy took a skip and a jump down the lane.

definitions.definition: An object which causes one to jump, a ramp.
definitions.example: He went off a jump.

definitions.definition: An instance of causing oneself to fall from an elevated location.
definitions.example: There were a couple of jumps from the bridge.

definitions.definition: An instance of employing a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
definitions.example: She was terrified before the jump, but was thrilled to be skydiving.

definitions.definition: An instance of reacting to a sudden stimulus by jerking the body.
A jumping move in a board game.
definitions.example: the knight's jump in chess

definitions.definition: A button (of a joypad, joystick or similar device) used to make a video game character jump (propel itself upwards).
definitions.example: Press jump to start.

definitions.definition: An obstacle that forms part of a showjumping course, and that the horse has to jump over cleanly.
definitions.example: Heartless managed the scale the first jump but fell over the second.

definitions.definition: (with on) An early start or an advantage.
definitions.example: He got a jump on the day because he had laid out everything the night before.

definitions.definition: A discontinuity in the graph of a function, where the function is continuous in a punctured interval of the discontinuity.
An instance of faster-than-light travel, not observable from ordinary space.
A change of the path of execution to a different location.
Short for jump-start.
definitions.example: My car won't start. Could you give me a jump?

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.
definitions.example: Kangaroos are known for their ability to jump high.

definitions.definition: To cause oneself to leave an elevated location and fall downward.
definitions.example: She is going to jump from the diving board.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.
definitions.example: to jump a stream

definitions.definition: To employ a parachute to leave an aircraft or elevated location.
To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.
definitions.example: The sudden sharp sound made me jump.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.
definitions.example: Share prices jumped by 10% after the company announced record profits.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.
definitions.example: I hate it when people jump the queue.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.
definitions.example: The hoodlum jumped a woman in the alley.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.
definitions.example: The rider jumped the horse over the fence.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.
To increase the height of a tower crane by inserting a section at the base of the tower and jacking up everything above it.
To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.
(smithwork) To join by a buttweld.
To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.
(quarrying) To bore with a jumper.
To coincide; to agree; to accord; to tally; followed by with.
To start executing code from a different location, rather than following the program counter.
To flee; to make one's escape.
Exact; matched; fitting; precise.
Exactly; precisely
An occasion when a performer or team of them (especially in vaudeville) expects to perform at a theater for a single evening.

A single sexual encounter between two individuals, where at least one of the partners has no immediate intention or expectation of establishing a longer-term sexual or romantic relationship. As the phrase implies, the relationship lasts for only one night.
Either of the two partners involved in such a single sexual encounter.
A newcomer, someone new to something.
A new user or participant; someone who is extremely new and inexperienced (to a game or activity). A beginner.
Anything recently introduced into a setting, especially something that replaces an older version.
Having a fast tempo.
Having an aggressive pace.
With a fast tempo.
With an aggressive pace.

definitions.definition: To shoot; to attempt to score a goal.

definitions.definition: To forcibly direct (something).

definitions.definition: To inflame; to irritate, as the passions.

definitions.definition: A two-dimensional geometric figure, a line, consisting of the set of all those points in a plane that are equally distant from a given point (center).
definitions.example: The set of all points (x, y) such that (x − 1)2 + y2 = r2 is a circle of radius r around the point (1, 0).

definitions.definition: A two-dimensional geometric figure, a disk, consisting of the set of all those points of a plane at a distance less than or equal to a fixed distance (radius) from a given point.
Any shape, curve or arrangement of objects that approximates to or resembles the geometric figures.
definitions.example: Children, please join hands and form a circle.

definitions.definition: A specific group of persons; especially one who shares a common interest.

definitions.definition: The orbit of an astronomical body.
A line comprising two semicircles of 30 yards radius centred on the wickets joined by straight lines parallel to the pitch used to enforce field restrictions in a one-day match.
A ritual circle that is cast three times deosil and closes three times widdershins either in the air with a wand or literally with stones or other items used for worship.
A traffic circle or roundabout.
Compass; circuit; enclosure.

An instrument of observation, whose graduated limb consists of an entire circle. When fixed to a wall in an observatory, it is called a mural circle; when mounted with a telescope on an axis and in Y's, in the plane of the meridian, a meridian or transit circle; when involving the principle of reflection, like the sextant, a reflecting circle; and when that of repeating an angle several times continuously along the graduated limb, a repeating circle.
A series ending where it begins, and repeating itself.
A form of argument in which two or more unproved statements are used to prove each other; inconclusive reasoning.
Indirect form of words; circumlocution.
A territorial division or district.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) A bagginess of the skin below the eyes from lack of sleep.

definitions.definition: To travel around along a curved path.

Black rent, or rent paid in corn, meat, or the lowest coin, as opposed to white rent, which was paid in silver.
Compromising material that can be used to extort someone, dirt.

Prone to treat someone badly by coarse, insulting words or other maltreatment; vituperative; reproachful; scurrilous.
